scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced accepted plant work through explicit lifecycle states and tied completion to batch, permit, calibration, sample, and turnover evidence.
Restart handling stayed blocked when jurisdiction, permit scope, system links, and authority maps were missing.
Missing field-round, lineup, and lab hold-release artifacts prevented mid-process progression and preserved a rework or hold posture.
Off-spec samples, analyzer drift, and open equipment defects were treated as answer-changing execution exceptions instead of minor notes.
Completion was refused when sample approval, cleanup verification, and turnover evidence were absent.
Permit, threshold, monitoring, and inspection evidence gaps blocked throughput escalation and triggered a precise evidence request.
Changed field conditions reopened readiness and escalated the case without relying on stale prior approval posture.
Engineering and safety-critical signoff requests were refused cleanly and routed to the proper authority owners.
Conflicting public baseline and tenant SOP requirements were preserved and escalated instead of over-resolved.
Incident reporting, maintenance workaround approval, and regulator-facing ownership were rejected and routed to adjacent specialists.
Tenant-specific line-up, sample-hold, and startup controls were applied explicitly while the public baseline remained visible.
Chemical plant terminology was normalized into authoritative records before lifecycle and blocker decisions were made.
New environmental monitoring failures and inspection findings reversed prior readiness and escalated regulated-risk handling.

notes: All 13 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 13 chemical plant operations scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no engineering, safety-critical, environmental-certification, or regulator-facing authority is claimed.
